tgp_track_required_for_laser.canonical: TGP must be in TRACK mode to fire laser
tgp_track_required_for_laser.claim: The AN/ASQ-228 ATFLIR (TGP) laser will only fire when the pod is actively tracking a designation — either POINT TRACK or AREA TRACK. In SEARCH mode the laser trigger is inhibited regardless of Laser Arm state.

laser_arm_separate_from_master_arm.canonical: Laser Arm is a separate switch from Master Arm
laser_arm_separate_from_master_arm.claim: The F/A-18C has two independent arm switches on the Master Arm panel: Master Arm (controls all weapon releases) and Laser Arm (controls TGP laser emission). Both must be set to ARM for laser-guided weapons. Setting Master Arm to ARM does not automatically arm the laser.

radar_stby_does_not_emit.canonical: Radar in STBY does not emit RF energy
radar_stby_does_not_emit.claim: When the APG-73 radar is in STBY mode, it is powered and warmed up but does not transmit RF energy. It is not detectable by threat radar warning receivers. Switching to an operating mode (RWS, TWS, STT, VS) begins emission. EMERG mode forces full power regardless of safety interlocks.

aim120_pitbull_range.canonical: AIM-120 active seeker activates at approximately 10-15 nm from target
aim120_pitbull_range.claim: The AIM-120C AMRAAM relies on radar uplink from the launching aircraft until its onboard active seeker goes autonomous (pitbull). In DCS this typically occurs 10-15 nm from the target depending on altitude and closure rate. After pitbull the pilot may crank or notch without the missile losing guidance.

master_arm_required_for_all_weapons.canonical: Master Arm must be ARM to release any weapon including the gun
master_arm_required_for_all_weapons.claim: No weapon — gun, missile, or bomb — can be released or fired unless the Master Arm switch is in ARM. The gun additionally requires the Gun Arm switch (also on the Master Arm panel) to be ARM. The system will not generate a firing signal with Master Arm in SAFE.

gbu12_min_release_altitude.canonical: GBU-12 minimum safe release altitude is 1500 ft AGL
gbu12_min_release_altitude.claim: The GBU-12 Paveway II requires a minimum of approximately 1500 ft AGL to allow the fuze to arm during fall time. Releasing below this altitude risks unexploded ordnance or immediate detonation risk to the aircraft. Optimal release altitude for a medium-angle delivery is 10,000-15,000 ft AGL.
